subject: Economics
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze how investment in education and health shifts a population from a liability to an asset
Evaluate the 'Virtuous' vs. 'Vicious' cycles of human development
Explain the structural differences between the Primary, Secondary, and Tertiary sectors
Investigate the determinants of earnings in the labor market (Education and Skill)
Analyze the impact of Disguised and Seasonal unemployment on the rural economy
Assess the socio-economic consequences of unemployment

lesson_plan.title: Human Capital Formation & Investment
topics_covered: Human Capital as a superior resource
Comparison of Human vs. Physical vs. Land resources
Case studies: Investment in Japan vs. India
teaching_strategy: Comparative analysis of global economies (e.g., Japan's growth)
Discussion on the return on investment (ROI) in humans
student_activities: Draft a comparison table: Human vs. Physical Capital
Brainstorm why Japan is rich despite having few natural resources
assessment: Short analytical note on 'Human Resource Superiority'

lesson_plan.title: The Virtuous vs. Vicious Cycle
topics_covered: The impact of parent's education on the next generation
Breaking the cycle of poverty through state intervention
Detailed comparison: Sakal vs. Vilas
teaching_strategy: Flowcharting the two cycles on the board
student_activities: Create a flowchart of a 'Virtuous Cycle' and a 'Vicious Cycle'
Write a 100-word analysis on the role of Sakal’s vocational training
assessment: Peer review of the Sakal/Vilas analysis

lesson_plan.title: Sectors of the Economy & National Income
topics_covered: Interdependence of Primary, Secondary, and Tertiary sectors
Market vs. Non-market activities
Women's labor and the National Income gap
teaching_strategy: Discussion on why domestic work is excluded from GDP
student_activities: Debate: 'Should household work be recognized in National Income?'

lesson_plan.title: Quality of Population: Education Policy
topics_covered: Trends in literacy: Gender and Regional gaps
Government expenditure on education (GDP percentage)
Vocationalization and Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan
teaching_strategy: Statistical analysis of literacy rate tables (1951-2011)
Lecture on the 'Bridge Course' and 'Back-to-school' camps
student_activities: Interpret a line graph showing the literacy gap between males and females
Identify the goals of the 12th Five-Year Plan for education

lesson_plan.title: Quality of Population: Health & Productivity
topics_covered: Life Expectancy and IMR as indicators of development
National Health Policy: Access to rural and underprivileged groups
The health-productivity link
teaching_strategy: Discussion on the 'Asset' value of a healthy worker
student_activities: Calculate the change in IMR over the last three decades using textbook data
Write a report on local health infrastructure in their district

lesson_plan.title: Unemployment: Structural & Rural Issues
topics_covered: Disguised unemployment in agriculture
Seasonal unemployment in rural labor
Educated unemployment in urban areas
student_activities: Solve a problem set on marginal productivity in farming
Map out the causes of educated unemployment in cities
assessment: Identification of unemployment types from case studies

lesson_plan.title: Consequences of Unemployment & Revision
topics_covered: Wastage of manpower resource
Economic overload on the working population
Summary of the whole chapter
student_activities: Participate in a group debate on reducing urban unemployment
Complete a summary mind map in notebooks

teaching_aids: Case study handouts on global human capital
teacher_tip: Use the story of Japan's success to emphasize that education and health can overcome a lack of natural resources; it shifts the focus from 'what we have' to 'what we can do'.
